questions.q: If A and B are square matrices of the same order, then (AB) ' =
questions.explanation: Since A and B are square matrices of the same order, the product AB is defined. The transpose of a product of matrices is the product of the transposes in reverse order, i.e., (AB)' = B'A'.

questions.q: If the determinant of a 2x2 matrix [a, b; c, d] is 6, then the determinant of its inverse is
questions.explanation: The determinant of the inverse of a matrix A is the reciprocal of the determinant of matrix A. So, if the determinant of matrix A is 6, then the determinant of its inverse is 1/6.

questions.q: The determinant of a matrix A = |a, b; c, d| is 12. Find the determinant of the matrix A^2.
questions.explanation: The determinant of the product of two matrices A and B is equal to the product of the determinants of A and B, i.e., |A^2| = |A| * |A| = 12 * 12 = 144.

questions.q: The determinant of a 3x3 matrix A is 5. Find the determinant of the matrix A^(-1).
questions.explanation: The determinant of the inverse of a matrix A is the reciprocal of the determinant of matrix A. So, if the determinant of matrix A is 5, then the determinant of its inverse is 1/5.

questions.q: If A is a square matrix of order n x n, then |kA| =
questions.explanation: The determinant of a matrix A multiplied by a scalar k is equal to k^n times the determinant of matrix A, i.e., |kA| = k^n |A|.

questions.q: The determinant of the matrix A = |a, b; c, d| is 18. Find the determinant of the matrix 2A.
questions.explanation: The determinant of the matrix 2A is equal to 2^2 times the determinant of matrix A, i.e., |2A| = 4 * 18 = 72.

questions.q: If A is a square matrix of order n x n, then |A| = |A'|.
questions.explanation: The determinant of a matrix A is equal to the determinant of its transpose A'.

questions.q: The determinant of the matrix A = |a, 0; 0, b| is 8. Find the determinant of the matrix B = |b, 0; 0, a|.
questions.explanation: The determinant of matrix B is equal to the determinant of matrix A, i.e., |B| = |A| = 8.

questions.q: If the determinant of a 2x2 matrix [a, b; c, d] is -6, then the determinant of the matrix [d, -b; -c, a] is
questions.explanation: The determinant of the matrix [d, -b; -c, a] is equal to the determinant of the matrix [a, b; c, d] multiplied by (-1)^2, i.e., |d, -b; -c, a| = |a, b; c, d| = -6.

questions.q: The determinant of the matrix A = |1, 2; 3, 4| is 2. Find the determinant of the matrix B = |4, 1; 2, 3|.
questions.explanation: The determinant of the matrix B is equal to the determinant of the matrix A multiplied by (-1)^3, i.e., |B| = (-1)^3 * |A| = -2.

questions.q: If A is a square matrix of order n x n, then |A| = 0 implies A is singular.
questions.explanation: A square matrix A is said to be singular if its determinant is zero. If |A| = 0, then the matrix A is singular.

questions.q: The determinant of the matrix A = |a, 0; 0, b| is 8. Find the determinant of the matrix B = |0, 0; 0, 0|.
questions.explanation: The determinant of the matrix B is equal to 0, i.e., |B| = 0.

questions.q: If A is a square matrix of order n x n, then |kA| = k^n |A|.
questions.explanation: The determinant of a matrix A multiplied by a scalar k is equal to k^n times the determinant of matrix A, i.e., |kA| = k^n |A|.

questions.q: The determinant of the matrix A = |1, 2; 3, 4| is 2. Find the determinant of the matrix B = |1, 4; 3, 2|.
questions.explanation: The determinant of the matrix B is equal to the determinant of the matrix A multiplied by (-1)^3, i.e., |B| = (-1)^3 * |A| = -2.

questions.q: The determinant of the matrix A = |a, b; c, d| is 12. Find the determinant of the matrix B = |d, -b; -c, a|.
questions.explanation: The determinant of the matrix B is equal to the determinant of the matrix A, i.e., |B| = |A| = 12.

questions.q: If A is a square matrix of order n x n, then |A| = |A^(-1)|.
questions.explanation: The determinant of the inverse of a matrix A is the reciprocal of the determinant of matrix A, i.e., |A^(-1)| = 1/|A|.

questions.q: The determinant of the matrix A = |1, 2, 3; 4, 5, 6; 7, 8, 9| is -720. Find the determinant of the matrix B = |6, 3, 2; 7, 4, 1; 8, 5, 9|.
questions.explanation: The determinant of the matrix B is equal to the determinant of the matrix A multiplied by (-1)^6, i.e., |B| = (-1)^6 * |A| = -(-720) = 720.

questions.q: If A is a square matrix of order n x n, then |A| = |A^T|.
questions.explanation: The determinant of a matrix A is equal to the determinant of its transpose A'.
